summary: Wave 13 is mostly broad MTA meeting material, not a bus-priority-specific source set. The completed records are useful as searchable canonical context, but most writer batches should be deferred unless they directly improve bus-priority project, corridor, route, or enforcement pages.
low_value_writer_candidates: Most entity-heavy meeting pages, railroad capital/procurement pages, generic finance/budget variance pages, board/person entity pages, and source-gap pages about preliminary monthly metrics are low-value for bus-priority prose unless they connect directly to a bus project or treatment.

summary: Added concise, source-grounded writer-region context only on owned high-confidence bus-priority pages: ABLE procurement/ACE scope, Queens Bus Network Redesign hearing/budget/implementation context, and Gun Hill Road bus-lane corridor context.
Wave 13 had 64 quarantined sources, all reported as ingest timeouts after 2100000ms. This Codex-only pass did not retry them or run provider-backed recovery commands.

Several wave 13 bus-related records look high-signal but should be reviewed manually or in a future allowed canonical identity workflow before broad writer expansion. The ABLE/ACE and Gun Hill records include likely duplicate or near-duplicate page-bearing identities; Queens records include useful budget/staffing and implementation facts already surfaced on the owned page.
suggested_action: Prioritize deterministic/manual identity review for duplicate ABLE/ACE and Gun Hill project records before adding prose to their many generated pages. Keep broad meeting-doc finance, rail, bridge, entity, and preliminary-metric pages as canonical search context unless a bus-priority page needs the cited fact.

summary: A few source IDs listed as quarantined in the wave 13 report already appear in the current canonical/wiki state, so the dirty worktree should be treated as mixed-state rather than a pure snapshot of the wave report.
details: Examples inspected during this pass: meeting_doc_140461 appears on the ABLE and Queens Bus Network Redesign pages; meeting_doc_157946 backs ACE and QBNR budget/staffing claims; meeting_doc_163121 backs QBNR approval/implementation and bus performance records. This pass used those existing canonical/source-block records where source-grounded, but did not run ingest, materialize, or dedup recovery.
suggested_action: Before any automated recovery or writer expansion, reconcile wave report quarantine status against canonical/source-page provenance for these IDs and avoid assuming quarantined means absent from the current corpus.
